name: Adults And Youth United Development Association Inc
description_short: Bilingual nonprofit providing housing assistance, emergency rental support, financial counseling, and community development services to low-income El Paso County families since 1992.
description_long:
Adults and Youth United Development Association, Inc. (A.Y.U.D.A Inc.) was founded in 1992 as a grassroots, bilingual nonprofit organization dedicated to improving quality of life for low-income individuals and families in El Paso County. The organization operates on principles of self-sufficiency and community empowerment, addressing structural barriers to safe housing, clean water, quality education, environmental health, and nutrition.

A.Y.U.D.A offers an extensive portfolio of housing and financial assistance programs funded through federal and state grants. Their housing services include the Homeowner Assistance Fund (HAF) for mortgage and property tax assistance, the Amy Young Barrier Removal Program, emergency rental assistance (ERA1 and ERA2), home modification programs for older adults and veterans, weatherization assistance, and contract-for-deed support. Beyond housing, they provide financial counseling, community health worker training, advocacy services, and AmeriCorps NCCC placements. The organization has served thousands of single-family households since 2012, with documented impact across multiple program areas.

A.Y.U.D.A distinguishes itself through its deeply rooted community presence in El Paso County, bilingual service delivery, and integration of multiple service lines addressing interconnected poverty issues. The organization combines direct financial assistance with skill-building programs (like health worker certification and the Mujeres Empoderadas initiative) and community organizing through advocacy and colonias resilience work. Their partnerships with government agencies and community organizations demonstrate institutional trust and capacity.

The primary limitation is geographic scope—services are concentrated in El Paso County, Texas, making this organization inaccessible to consumers outside that region. While their website documents program names and impact numbers, specific eligibility criteria, application processes, and service availability details are not fully transparent online. The organization's reliance on grant funding means program availability may fluctuate, and processing times for assistance are not publicly specified.
pros: Bilingual services addressing El Paso County's demographic needs
Comprehensive housing assistance including mortgage/property tax help (HAF) with 1,075 families served
Emergency rental assistance programs (ERA1 and ERA2) providing direct aid
Financial counseling and skill-building programs beyond basic assistance
Free services funded by grants—no client fees or predatory terms
AmeriCorps NCCC program providing paid community service opportunities
Documented track record since 1992 with measurable community impact statistics
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to El Paso County, Texas only
Website lacks specific eligibility requirements and application instructions for most programs
Program availability dependent on grant funding cycles, creating potential service gaps
No online estimate tools or transparent timeline information for assistance processing
Contact information and intake procedures not clearly visible on homepage
best_for: Low-income El Paso County residents needing affordable housing solutions or homeownership preservation
Spanish-speaking families seeking bilingual financial counseling and rental assistance
Homeowners facing mortgage/property tax difficulties in Texas
Older adults and veterans seeking home modification support
diagnosis: A.Y.U.D.A is ideal for low-income El Paso County residents needing free, grant-funded housing assistance, emergency rental support, or financial counseling with bilingual service delivery. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation—this organization serves only El Paso County, Texas and cannot assist consumers elsewhere.
services: Homeowner Assistance Fund (HAF) - mortgage and property tax assistance
Amy Young Barrier Removal Program - housing accessibility
Emergency Rental Assistance Program (ERA1 and ERA2)
Housing for Texas Heroes home modification grants
Community Health Worker/Promotor(a) Training and Certification
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1325 Beverly Ann Dr, San Elizario, TX 79849
